{{Expand}}

{{Infobox concept
| name = investing
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Investing is the process of charging an object with a person's [[Intent]]. This occurs naturally and unavoidably whenever a human uses a tool, such as using a coat for warmth or a brush for grooming. By willing an object to perform its intended function more effectively, the user allows the item to absorb their [[Intent]], which enhances its capabilities. For example, a knife might become much sharper for cutting meat, or glass might gain the strength to withstand heavy hail. Over long periods of use, tools like axes can accumulate a measurable amount of [[Intent]] that makes them excel at their specific tasks. While most people perform this action blindly, specialized individuals known as [[Readers]] can perceive the actual changes made by the [[Intent]]. The process can be complex, sometimes requiring a person to focus their [[Intent]] on multiple components simultaneously, which can cause significant physical strain. If a person focuses too intensely, they risk investing themselves. While some modern perspectives refer to this phenomenon as empowering, the term investing is used to describe the act of lending [[Intent]] to an item.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Charging an object with [[Intent]] is called '[[investing]]'.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c4">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire concepts]]
